sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a frequent water skier, reports experiencing right knee pain since a skiing accident five days ago, where he twisted his knee while landing from a jump.
He describes the pain as sore to climb stairs and squat, but denies any numbness or tingling in the foot.
He has been taking over-the-counter Aleve twice a day and applying a cold pack, which has provided some relief.
He has a history of high blood pressure, for which he is taking lisinopril 20mg daily, and has been managing his blood pressure well, with readings of 125/80 at home.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of having his tonsils removed and no other significant medical conditions.
He is a non-smoker, and his social history is notable for frequent water skiing and occasional baseball watching.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms or complaints.
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure is 120/77.
Physical exam: The patient has ecchymosis and edema on the right knee, with an effusion present.
There is pain to palpation of the right medial knee, and the patient has decreased range of motion.
The varus and valgus test, Lachman sign, and dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulse are all negative.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain in the right knee.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain, right knee; Hypertension; Past: tonsillectomy.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg once a day to help reduce swelling and pain in the right knee.
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any fractures.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent future injuries.
I continued the patient on lisinopril 20mg daily for his hypertension and ordered a lipid panel to monitor his lipid levels.
I administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: The patient will return for follow-up after the results of the x-ray are available, and I will reassess his knee and provide further instructions.
